AMD.reasoning: Branch A fires — Fade the Extreme. AMD is in a statistically severe overbought condition across every measured dimension: daily Z-score of 2.6 (above the +2 threshold), RSI-14 at 83.06 and RSI-21 at 86.46 (both well above the 75 overbought threshold), and price is trading 52.6% above the 50-SMA and 27.6% above the 20-SMA — both extreme extensions far beyond the 10% noteworthy threshold. Bollinger %B at 0.9294 places price approaching the upper band (upper at 367.10) but not yet pierced through it. Daily ADX at 74.94 confirms a very strong trend, which ordinarily argues against fading — but the magnitude of the extension makes snapback risk high regardless. Wiki context: as of 2026-04-17, AMD had RSI of 91 in a prior overbought episode; the system flagged it and sized small. That prior extreme resolved somewhat before the stock extended further, making this at least a second major overbought episode — repeat attempts at the same overbought zone slightly lower conviction for an immediate fade. Branch B does NOT fire: price is at the recent swing high (354.96), not in a pullback within an uptrend. Confidence is moderated (62) because: (1) no reversal candle confirmation — price closed near the day's high; (2) hourly RSI at 66.57 is elevated but not extreme, and hourly MACD shows a bullish crossover, meaning short-term momentum has not yet rolled over; (3) hourly ADX of 17.25 (weak) suggests indecision but not reversal. Entry is set at current price for a fade, with a stop above the recent swing high plus 1 ATR (17.35), and a target at mean reversion to the Bollinger middle band / 20-SMA zone (~278).
